symbols.name: AppState
symbols.signature: export type AppState = DeepImmutable<{ settings, verbose, mainLoopModel, toolPermissionContext, mcp, plugins, tasks, sessionHooks, ... }> & { tasks: { [taskId: string]: TaskState }, mcp: {...}, plugins: {...}, ... }
symbols.purpose: The single source of truth for all reactive UI and session state, including settings, permission context, plugins, MCP connections, tasks, memory hooks, and feature flags.

symbols.name: AppStateStore
symbols.signature: export type AppStateStore = Store<AppState>
symbols.purpose: Type alias for the generic store parameterized with AppState.

symbols.name: getDefaultAppState
symbols.signature: export function getDefaultAppState(): AppState
symbols.purpose: Constructs the initial AppState with all defaults, including loading initial settings and computing initial permission mode from teammate context.

symbols.name: createStore
symbols.signature: export function createStore<T>(initialState: T, onChange?: OnChange<T>): Store<T>
symbols.purpose: Minimal pub-sub store implementation: holds state, exposes setState with Object.is diffing, and notifies listeners plus an optional onChange side-effect callback.

symbols.name: Store
symbols.signature: export type Store<T> = { getState: () => T; setState: (updater: (prev: T) => T) => void; subscribe: (listener: Listener) => () => void }
symbols.purpose: Generic observable state container interface used as the backbone for AppState.

symbols.name: AppStateProvider
symbols.signature: export function AppStateProvider({ children, initialState, onChangeAppState }): React.ReactNode
symbols.purpose: React provider that creates the AppStateStore, prevents nesting, integrates settings change watching, and wraps children with MailboxProvider and VoiceProvider.

symbols.name: onChangeAppState
symbols.signature: export function onChangeAppState({ newState, oldState }: { newState: AppState; oldState: AppState }): void
symbols.purpose: Side-effect dispatcher wired to the store's onChange callback: syncs permission mode to CCR/SDK, persists model and UI settings to disk, clears auth caches on settings change.

symbols.name: selectors
symbols.signature: export function getViewedTeammateTask(...): InProcessTeammateTaskState | undefined; export function getActiveAgentForInput(appState: AppState): ActiveAgentForInput
symbols.purpose: Pure derived-state functions extracting teammate and agent routing information from AppState without side effects.

symbols.name: loadMemoryPrompt
symbols.signature: export async function loadMemoryPrompt(): Promise<string | null>
symbols.purpose: Dispatches to the correct memory prompt builder based on enabled features (KAIROS daily-log, TEAMMEM combined, or standard auto-memory), returning null when memory is disabled.

symbols.name: buildMemoryPrompt
symbols.signature: export function buildMemoryPrompt({ displayName, memoryDir, extraGuidelines? }): string
symbols.purpose: Constructs the complete memory system prompt including behavioral instructions, type taxonomy, and current MEMORY.md content (synchronously read from disk).

symbols.name: buildMemoryLines
symbols.signature: export function buildMemoryLines(displayName, memoryDir, extraGuidelines?, skipIndex?): string[]
symbols.purpose: Produces the instructional lines of the memory system prompt without injecting MEMORY.md content, used by both agent memory and the system prompt path.

symbols.name: truncateEntrypointContent
symbols.signature: export function truncateEntrypointContent(raw: string): EntrypointTruncation
symbols.purpose: Applies the 200-line and 25KB byte caps to MEMORY.md content, appending a warning that names which cap fired.

symbols.name: ensureMemoryDirExists
symbols.signature: export async function ensureMemoryDirExists(memoryDir: string): Promise<void>
symbols.purpose: Idempotently creates the memory directory (recursive mkdir), called once per session before prompt building so the model can always write without checking existence.

symbols.name: isAutoMemoryEnabled
symbols.signature: export function isAutoMemoryEnabled(): boolean
symbols.purpose: Checks a priority chain (env var CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY → --bare/SIMPLE → CCR without persistent storage → settings.json) to determine whether the memory system is active.

symbols.name: getAutoMemPath
symbols.signature: export const getAutoMemPath = memoize((): string => {...}, () => getProjectRoot())
symbols.purpose: Resolves the auto-memory directory via: Cowork env override → settings.json autoMemoryDirectory → ~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-git-root>/memory/.

symbols.name: isAutoMemPath
symbols.signature: export function isAutoMemPath(absolutePath: string): boolean
symbols.purpose: Security guard checking whether a path falls inside the auto-memory directory to grant filesystem write carve-outs to memory files.

symbols.name: validateMemoryPath
symbols.signature: function validateMemoryPath(raw: string | undefined, expandTilde: boolean): string | undefined
symbols.purpose: Rejects dangerous memory path overrides (relative, root/near-root, UNC, Windows drive-root, null-byte) before they can become filesystem allow-list roots.

symbols.name: MemoryType
symbols.signature: export type MemoryType = 'user' | 'feedback' | 'project' | 'reference'
symbols.purpose: Closed four-type taxonomy for persistent memory, explicitly excluding derivable code-state information.

symbols.name: scanMemoryFiles
symbols.signature: export async function scanMemoryFiles(memoryDir: string, signal: AbortSignal): Promise<MemoryHeader[]>
symbols.purpose: Recursively reads .md memory files, parses frontmatter for type/description, sorts newest-first, and caps at 200 files.

symbols.name: findRelevantMemories
symbols.signature: export async function findRelevantMemories(query, memoryDir, signal, recentTools?, alreadySurfaced?): Promise<RelevantMemory[]>
symbols.purpose: Two-stage recall: scans memory file headers, then asks a Sonnet side-query to select up to 5 relevant files, returning paths with mtimes for freshness display.

symbols.name: memoryFreshnessText
symbols.signature: export function memoryFreshnessText(mtimeMs: number): string
symbols.purpose: Generates a staleness caveat for memories older than 1 day, warning the model to verify code citations before asserting as fact.

symbols.name: PermissionMode
symbols.signature: export type PermissionMode = 'default' | 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions' | 'dontAsk' | 'auto' | 'bubble'
symbols.purpose: Discriminated union controlling how tool permission decisions are made; 'auto' and 'bubble' are ant-only internal modes not exposed externally.

symbols.name: PermissionRule
symbols.signature: export type PermissionRule = { behavior: PermissionBehavior; ruleValue: PermissionRuleValue; source: PermissionRuleSource }
symbols.purpose: A single allow/deny/ask rule binding a behavior to a tool name and optional content pattern, tagged with its originating settings source.

symbols.name: hasPermissionsToUseTool
symbols.signature: export const hasPermissionsToUseTool: CanUseToolFn = async (tool, input, context, assistantMessage, toolUseID): Promise<PermissionDecision>
symbols.purpose: Core permission check function that evaluates rules, applies mode transformations (dontAsk→deny, auto→classifier), and handles denial tracking fallback to prompting.

symbols.name: applyPermissionRulesToPermissionContext
symbols.signature: export function applyPermissionRulesToPermissionContext(context: ToolPermissionContext, rules: PermissionRule[]): ToolPermissionContext
symbols.purpose: Merges a flat rule list into the permission context's alwaysAllowRules/alwaysDenyRules/alwaysAskRules collections by behavior.

symbols.name: DenialTrackingState
symbols.signature: export type DenialTrackingState = { consecutiveDenials: number; totalDenials: number }
symbols.purpose: Session-scoped counter for classifier denials, used to fall back to interactive prompting when limits (3 consecutive or 20 total) are exceeded.

symbols.name: DANGEROUS_FILES
symbols.signature: export const DANGEROUS_FILES = ['.gitconfig', '.bashrc', '.zshrc', '.mcp.json', '.claude.json', ...]
symbols.purpose: Allowlist of sensitive files protected from auto-edit, checked in write permission guards.

symbols.name: DANGEROUS_DIRECTORIES
symbols.signature: export const DANGEROUS_DIRECTORIES = ['.git', '.vscode', '.idea', '.claude']
symbols.purpose: Directories blocked from auto-editing; .claude is included to prevent settings/hooks from being overwritten without permission.

symbols.name: checkWritePermissionForTool
symbols.signature: export function checkWritePermissionForTool<Input extends AnyObject>(...): PermissionResult
symbols.purpose: Filesystem-level write permission check that validates paths against working directories, dangerous files/dirs, memory path carve-outs, and session-scoped allow rules.

symbols.name: SettingSource
symbols.signature: export type SettingSource = 'userSettings' | 'projectSettings' | 'localSettings' | 'flagSettings' | 'policySettings'
symbols.purpose: Ordered discriminated union of all settings origins; later sources override earlier ones in merge order.

symbols.name: settingsChangeDetector
symbols.signature: export const settingsChangeDetector = { initialize, dispose, subscribe, notifyChange, resetForTesting }
symbols.purpose: Chokidar-based file watcher for settings JSON files plus an MDM poll (30min interval), emits SettingSource events to subscribers on external changes.

symbols.name: applySettingsChange
symbols.signature: export function applySettingsChange(source: SettingSource, setAppState: (f: (prev: AppState) => AppState) => void): void
symbols.purpose: Applies a settings file change to AppState: reads fresh settings, reloads permission rules and hooks config snapshot, strips dangerous Bash rules, and handles bypass-permissions killswitch.

symbols.name: resetSettingsCache
symbols.signature: export function resetSettingsCache(): void
symbols.purpose: Clears all three settings caches (session merged, per-source, per-file) atomically; called once by the change detector before notifying N listeners to avoid N-way disk re-reads.

symbols.name: GlobalConfig
symbols.signature: export type GlobalConfig = { userID?, theme, numStartups, autoUpdates?, oauthAccount?, primaryApiKey?, verbose, env, autoCompactEnabled, ... }
symbols.purpose: User-global persistent configuration stored in ~/.claude/claude.json, covering UI preferences, API keys, update settings, and session-agnostic preferences.

symbols.name: ProjectConfig
symbols.signature: export type ProjectConfig = { allowedTools, mcpContextUris, mcpServers?, lastAPIDuration?, hasTrustDialogAccepted?, activeWorktreeSession?, ... }
symbols.purpose: Per-project persistent configuration embedded in GlobalConfig.projects map, tracking MCP settings, session metrics, trust dialog state, and worktree sessions.

symbols.name: getProjectDir
symbols.signature: export const getProjectDir = memoize((projectDir: string): string => join(getProjectsDir(), sanitizePath(projectDir)))
symbols.purpose: Memoized function that maps a cwd string to its sanitized project storage directory under ~/.claude/projects/.

symbols.name: getTranscriptPath
symbols.signature: export function getTranscriptPath(): string
symbols.purpose: Returns the JSONL transcript file path for the current session, honoring session project directory override set on resume/branch.

symbols.name: buildConversationChain
symbols.signature: export function buildConversationChain(messages: Map<UUID, TranscriptMessage>, leafMessage: TranscriptMessage): TranscriptMessage[]
symbols.purpose: Reconstructs the conversation chain by walking parentUuid pointers from leaf to root, with DAG recovery for parallel tool-use sibling messages.

symbols.name: recordTranscript
symbols.signature: export async function recordTranscript(messages, teamInfo?, startingParentUuidHint?, allMessages?): Promise<UUID | null>
symbols.purpose: Appends new messages to the session JSONL, deduplicating already-recorded messages while preserving parentUuid chain integrity across compaction.

symbols.name: SessionHooksState
symbols.signature: export type SessionHooksState = Map<string, SessionStore>
symbols.purpose: In-memory Map of session-scoped hooks (keyed by sessionId) holding per-event matchers for both HookCommand (file-based) and FunctionHook (callback-based) hooks; deliberately uses Map to avoid O(N²) updates under parallel agent spawning.

symbols.name: loadAllPlugins
symbols.signature: export const loadAllPlugins = memoize(async (): Promise<PluginLoadResult> => {...})
symbols.purpose: Memoized full plugin load: clones/fetches marketplaces, assembles enabled/disabled sets with dependency resolution, caches to session, and warms the cache-only variant.

symbols.name: performBackgroundPluginInstallations
symbols.signature: export async function performBackgroundPluginInstallations(setAppState: SetAppState): Promise<void>
symbols.purpose: Background reconciler that diffs declared vs materialized marketplaces, installs/updates them with progress events reflected to AppState, and auto-refreshes plugins or sets needsRefresh.

symbols.name: useSettings
symbols.signature: export function useSettings(): ReadonlySettings
symbols.purpose: React hook providing reactive access to AppState.settings; updates whenever settings change on disk via the change detector pipeline.

symbols.name: useSettingsChange
symbols.signature: export function useSettingsChange(onChange: (source: SettingSource, settings: SettingsJson) => void): void
symbols.purpose: React hook subscribing to settingsChangeDetector events; reads fresh merged settings and invokes the callback on any file-system or MDM settings change.

symbols.name: useManagePlugins
symbols.signature: export function useManagePlugins({ enabled? }): void
symbols.purpose: React hook that performs the initial plugin load on mount (including delisted plugin enforcement and flagged notifications), and shows a notification when needsRefresh is set.

symbols.name: useCanUseTool
symbols.signature: function useCanUseTool(setToolUseConfirmQueue, setToolPermissionContext): CanUseToolFn
symbols.purpose: React hook producing the CanUseToolFn used by the query loop: checks permissions, handles interactive confirmation queue, routes to coordinator/swarm-worker/interactive handlers, and records classifier approvals.

data_flows.data: SettingSource identifier after external file change or MDM poll
Fresh SettingsJson merged from all sources
Updated settings + reloaded permission rules + hooks snapshot
{ newState, oldState } diff for side-effect dispatch
Persisted model selection, permission mode, UI preferences
Memory behavioral instructions + MEMORY.md index content
User query + memory manifest; returns selected filenames
Serialized Message objects with parentUuid chain

patterns.name: Centralized Observable Store with Side-Effect Bus
patterns.description: AppState is held in a single custom pub-sub store (createStore) rather than Redux or Zustand. The store's onChange callback (onChangeAppState) acts as a single choke point that reacts to any state diff and dispatches persistence, remote sync, and cache-busting side effects. This prevents the 'scattered callsites' problem where eight different permission-mode mutation paths previously failed to notify CCR.

patterns.name: Layered Settings with MDM/Policy Override
patterns.description: Settings are loaded from five sources in priority order (user < project < local < flag < policy). Policy (managed-settings.json + drop-in .d/ directory) is always included and always wins, enabling enterprise MDM control. A chokidar watcher plus a 30-minute MDM registry/plist poll keeps in-memory settings live without restart. The cache is reset atomically before notifying N listeners to avoid N-way disk re-reads.

patterns.name: File-Based Memory with Frontmatter Taxonomy and Sonnet Recall
patterns.description: Persistent memory is stored as Markdown files under ~/.claude/projects/<git-root>/memory/ with YAML frontmatter declaring a closed four-type taxonomy (user/feedback/project/reference). At query time, a secondary Sonnet model acts as a relevance selector: it reads file descriptions from a manifest and picks up to five files to inject. This two-stage architecture keeps the main context window clean while still surfacing relevant past context.

patterns.name: Pluggable Permission Modes with Classifier Auto-Approval
patterns.description: Permission evaluation is mode-parameterized: default requires interactive confirmation, bypassPermissions skips all checks, plan/acceptEdits auto-approve file edits, dontAsk converts ask→deny, and the ant-only 'auto' mode routes ask decisions to an AI transcript classifier. A denial-tracking state (stored in AppState) falls back to interactive prompting after 3 consecutive or 20 total classifier denials, preventing infinite loops.

patterns.name: Memoized Path Resolution with Security Validation
patterns.description: getAutoMemPath is memoized keyed on projectRoot to avoid repeated disk reads in hot render paths, but must also perform security validation (validateMemoryPath) to reject path traversal, UNC paths, Windows drive roots, and null bytes before the path can become a filesystem allow-list root. Memory path overrides from committed project settings (projectSettings) are explicitly excluded to prevent malicious repos from redirecting memory writes to ~/.ssh.

patterns.name: Plugin Load with Cache-Only Startup and Background Reconcile
patterns.description: The plugin loader has two memoized variants: loadAllPlugins (full, may clone git repos) and loadAllPluginsCacheOnly (reads from installed_plugins.json only). Startup uses cache-only to avoid blocking on git clones; a background reconciler (performBackgroundPluginInstallations) then installs/updates marketplaces and either auto-refreshes or sets needsRefresh for user-prompted reload.

patterns.name: SessionHooksState Uses Map Mutation to Avoid O(N²) Under Parallel Agents
patterns.description: Session hooks are stored in a Map (not a plain object) and mutators return the same Map reference after mutating it in place. Since store.ts uses Object.is diffing, same-reference mutations skip listener notification entirely. This is a deliberate performance optimization: parallel agent spawning with N schema-mode agents would otherwise fire N² Object.is comparisons and listener callbacks.

surprises: Memory path overrides from committed projectSettings are explicitly blocked to prevent a malicious repo's settings.json from silently redirecting memory writes to ~/.ssh or other sensitive directories.

The settings cache reset is centralized in the change detector's fanOut() rather than each subscriber, after profiling showed N subscribers × resetSettingsCache() caused N full disk reloads per notification event.

SessionHooksState deliberately uses Map mutation (returning same reference) to skip store listener notification under parallel agent spawning, avoiding O(N²) cost when N schema-mode agents register hooks in the same tick.

The ant-only 'auto' permission mode routes ask decisions to an AI transcript classifier instead of the user, with a hard fallback after 3 consecutive or 20 total classifier denials to prevent infinite loops.

Memory recall is a two-model pipeline: the main model sees only a MEMORY.md index (≤200 lines / 25KB), while a Sonnet side-query reads full file descriptions and selects ≤5 relevant files to inject — keeping main context window clean.

The KAIROS assistant-mode memory system diverges completely from standard memory: it uses append-only date-named log files (logs/YYYY/MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md) instead of maintaining a live MEMORY.md index, with a separate nightly /dream distillation process.

onChangeAppState wires a single diff-listener to the store's onChange callback rather than having 8+ scattered mutation sites each responsible for notifying CCR, fixing a bug where Shift+Tab cycling and plan-mode exit left the web UI's permission_mode stale.

Plugin startup uses a cache-only load path (loadAllPluginsCacheOnly) to avoid blocking on git clones, while a background reconciler runs separately; the two memoize caches are intentionally separate so a cache-only result never satisfies a caller needing fresh source.

The ManagedSettings drop-in directory (managed-settings.d/*.json) follows the systemd/sudoers drop-in convention: alphabetically sorted fragments are merged atop managed-settings.json, letting separate teams ship independent policy fragments without coordinating edits.

AppState includes a full REPL tool VM context (vm.Context + registered tools + console capture) stored on the state object, persisting the JavaScript execution environment across REPL calls within a session.
